scenarios.name: Quick Qualifier — Smooth Transfer

strengths: The SDR followed the script phases in order, ensuring a smooth conversation flow.
The SDR confirmed the prospect's last name, Barragan, to ensure accuracy.
The SDR asked relevant questions, such as about a checking or savings account, to qualify the prospect.
The SDR initiated the transfer with a licensed agent, using clear language.
The call was completed efficiently, in under 8 turns.
improvements_needed: The SDR's introduction could be more formal and clear, e.g., stating the company name and purpose.
The urgency created by the expiring offer could be more emphasized to prompt the prospect's decision.

scenarios.transcript:
SDR (Vicky): Hey Luis, how've you been?
Prospect (Luis): Uh, good I think. Who's this?
SDR (Vicky): This is Becky with the Benefits Review Team. The reason I'm calling — it looks like a while back you spoke with someone about getting a quote on final expense coverage, you know, for burial or cremation, and I just wanted to follow up on that. I have the last name here as Barragan. Is that right?
Prospect (Luis): That's right.
SDR (Vicky): Okay, yeah. So here's the thing. A preferred offer for your burial or cremation coverage was marked for you, and for whatever reason it was never claimed. With funeral costs running over nine thousand dollars these days, this is definitely worth looking at — and it actually expires tomorrow. Are you still interested in getting that quote before it expires?
Prospect (Luis): Yes, I'm interested.
SDR (Vicky): Okay, perfect. So one last thing, people who have a checking or savings account usually get the biggest discounts. Do you have one or the other?
Prospect (Luis): Yep, I have both.
SDR (Vicky): Okay, great. I have a licensed agent standing by to give you a quote. I'll have them jump on the call ASAP to walk you through all the details.

HOLD LINE 1: 
I'm seeing a preferred discounted offer attached to your profile that reflects the best pricing available today based on your age and health. That pricing window is expiring soon, so we want to make sure the agent reviews it with you before it updates.

HOLD LINE 2: 
The main thing with whole life insurance is making sure you have the right coverage and the right beneficiary so the money goes exactly where you want. The agent will walk you through all of that.

HOLD LINE 3: 
Just so you know, when the agent joins there might be a quick moment of silence as they jump in. As soon as you hear them, just let them know you're there and they'll take great care of you.

Great news — I have the agent on the line now. I'm going to hand you
Prospect (Luis): Okay.
SDR (Vicky): over. It was great talking with you, Luis. You're in good hands.

scenarios.name: Already Has Insurance

checkpoint_scores.comment: Agent acknowledged existing coverage positively: 'That's great you have something in place!'
Agent used comparison angle from FAQ: 'it's specifically for final expenses like burial or cremation, so your family isn't pulling from savings or your other policy for that'
Agent returned to script after handling objection: After prospect mentioned having life insurance, agent continued with 'This is actually about something a little different...'
Agent asked about checking/savings and initiated transfer: 'Do you have one or the other?' and then transferred the call

strengths: The agent effectively acknowledged the prospect's existing coverage and used it as a positive.
The agent used a relevant comparison angle to explain the purpose of the final expense coverage.
The agent successfully handled the objection and returned to the script.
The agent asked about checking/savings and initiated the transfer to a licensed agent.
improvements_needed: Consider a more personalized greeting instead of a generic 'Hey Harold, how've you been?'
The agent could have probed more to understand the prospect's current life insurance coverage before proceeding.

scenarios.transcript:
SDR (Vicky): Hey Harold, how've you been?
Prospect (Harold): Who is this?
SDR (Vicky): This is Becky with the Benefits Review Team. The reason I'm calling — it looks like a while back you spoke with someone about getting a quote on final expense coverage, you know, for burial or cremation, and I just wanted to follow up on that. I have the last name here as Peterson. Is that right?
Prospect (Harold): Yeah, that's me.
SDR (Vicky): Okay, yeah. So here's the thing. A preferred offer for your burial or cremation coverage was marked for you, and for whatever reason it was never claimed. With funeral costs running over nine thousand dollars these days, this is definitely worth looking at — and it actually expires tomorrow. Are you still interested in getting that quote before it expires?
Prospect (Harold): Well, I have life insurance. I have life insurance now. I'm covered with something.
SDR (Vicky): That's great you have something in place! Most folks I talk to do. This is actually about something a little different — it's specifically for final expenses like burial or cremation, so your family isn't pulling from savings or your other policy for that. The agent can show you how it works alongside what you already have. No obligation at all. Are you still interested in getting that quote before it expires?
Prospect (Harold): Okay. Go ahead.
SDR (Vicky): Okay, perfect. So one last thing, people who have a checking or savings account usually get the biggest discounts. Do you have one or the other?
Prospect (Harold): I have both.
SDR (Vicky): Great. I have a licensed agent standing by to give you a quote. I'll have them jump on the call ASAP to walk you through all the details.

HOLD LINE 1: I'm seeing a preferred discounted offer attached to your profile that reflects the best pricing available today based on your age and health. That pricing window is expiring soon, so we want to make sure the agent reviews it with you before it updates.

HOLD LINE 2: The main thing with whole life insurance is making sure you have the right coverage and the right beneficiary so the money goes exactly where you want. The agent will walk you through all of that.

HOLD LINE 3: Just so you know, when the agent joins there might be a quick moment of silence as they jump in. As soon as you hear them, just let them know you're there and they'll take great care of you.

Great news — I have the agent on the line now. I'm going to hand you over. It was great

scenarios.name: Not Interested — Firm No

checkpoint_scores.comment: Agent attempted soft objection handling but could be more convincing
Agent respected the second no and ended call politely
Agent did not push more than once

strengths: Respectful of prospect's time and decision
improvements_needed: More convincing soft objection handling approach

scenarios.transcript:
SDR (Vicky): Hey Frank, how've you been?
Prospect (Frank): Who's this?
SDR (Vicky): This is Becky with the Benefits Review Team. The reason I'm calling — it looks like a while back you spoke with someone about getting a quote on final expense coverage, you know, for burial or cremation, and I just wanted to follow up on that. I have the last name here as Davis. Is that right?
Prospect (Frank): Not interested.
SDR (Vicky): I understand, many people feel that way at first. Just so you know, we're offering free quotes with no obligation to buy, tailored to your budget and needs. Can I ask what concerns you most?
Prospect (Frank): No. I said I'm not interested. Take me off your list.
SDR (Vicky): I understand you're not interested. I'll make sure to note that and end the call. Have a great day.

scenarios.name: Asks For Price Before Transfer

checkpoint_scores.comment: Agent successfully deflected pricing to licensed agent
Agent used FAQ response about agent explaining costs
Agent kept prospect on the line during transfer
Agent did not give specific pricing numbers

strengths: The agent effectively introduced themselves and the purpose of the call
The agent confirmed the prospect's interest in getting a quote
The agent provided a smooth transfer to a licensed agent
improvements_needed: The agent could have provided more context about the preferred offer and the pricing discounts

scenarios.name: Voicemail — Answering Machine

checkpoint_scores.comment: Agent detected voicemail quickly and did not attempt to leave a lengthy message.
Agent hung up immediately after detecting voicemail.
Agent did not try to run the full script on a voicemail.

scenarios.transcript:
SDR (Vicky): Hey Patricia, how've you been?
Prospect (Patricia): Hi, you've reached Patricia. I'm not available right now. Please leave your name and number after the beep and I'll call you back. Thank you.

Beep.
SDR (Vicky): hang up immediately
